item.title: Oracle Warns Its Splurge on AI Data Centers May Not Pay Off
item.description: The company outlines the risks of its massive cloud build-out to meet AI demand

item.title: AI Productivity Hopes Collide With Job and Market Worries
item.description: OpenAI’s chief economist has soothing words on AI’s employment impact, but job cuts are happening.

item.title: USMCA Heads for Review as Trump Sours on His $2 Trillion Trade Deal
item.description: The 16-year pact has a six-year review clause which the US is expected to invoke

item.title: Ghana Inflation Rate Rises to Highest Level Since December
item.description: Ghana’s annual inflation rate climbed in June to the highest level so far this year, increasing the likelihood that the central bank will keep interest rates on hold when it meets later this month.

item.title: Wayve Files for Share Sale on London Bourse’s New Private Market
item.description: Autonomous driving software company Wayve Technologies Ltd. has filed to sell shares on the London Stock Exchange’s new Private Securities Market, the first major company to test the novel system.

item.title: AI, Forging New Risks, Isn’t a Market Wizard After All
item.description: More studies are trying to answer the question: what if everyone uses the same AI tool?

item.title: The World’s Second-Biggest Company Isn’t a ‘Rock Star’ Anymore
item.description: Google had a standout year, but now, a shift in the AI trade has it lagging the Nasdaq 100.

item.title: World Bank Backs Nigeria With $1.25 Billion Development Loan
item.description: The World Bank approved $1.25 billion in development financing to help Nigeria spur economic growth and create jobs after the West African nation embarked on reforms praised by the lender.

item.title: Goldman’s Oppenheimer Sees Stock Gains Broadening in Second Half
item.description: Global equities are set to extend their searing second-quarter rally, with earnings growth at technology companies a key driver, according to Peter Oppenheimer at Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.

item.title: ‘Taylor Swift Tax’ Stirs Bad Blood With Rhode Island Homeowners
item.description: Rhode Island’s new tax on second homes is nicknamed for its most famous part-time resident, music superstar Taylor Swift, but the levy will also squeeze owners of saltbox houses on the coast that are much less grand than the singer’s Holiday House mansion.

item.title: Wall Street’s AI Race Is Fueling New Fears of Crowded Trading
item.description: From hedge funds to wealth managers, Wall Street has embraced artificial intelligence in search of an investing edge. Researchers are now asking what happens when more investors turn to similar AI models to find one: buying the same stocks, reacting to the same headlines, and sometimes making the same mistakes.

item.title: Bad Loan Investor APS Eyes €3.3 Billion of Portfolio Deals
item.description: APS Holding is preparing to step up purchases of distressed debt as it sees higher interest rates and real estate stress starting to dent bank balance sheets.

item.title: UK Court Saves Former WH Smith Stores From Brink of Insolvency
item.description: Ailing UK high-street retailer TG Jones Retail Holdings Ltd won court approval for a sweeping financial restructuring that will shutter more than 100 stores and slash rents to stave off insolvency.

item.title: Tech and Finance Sectors Losing 28,000 Jobs Monthly Show AI Impact on Labor
item.description: Whether artificial intelligence will cause mass workforce cuts over time remains up for debate, but it is starting to leave an imprint on US employment data.

item.title: Trump Souring on USMCA Triggers Review of $2 Trillion Trade Deal
item.description: A countdown to the expiry of the US-Mexico-Canada Agreement is set to begin as officials launch a review of the sweeping trade deal that President Donald Trump signed during his first term.

item.title: Google and Actor Idris Elba to Fund African Creator Access to AI
item.description: Alphabet Inc.’s Google and UK actor Idris Elba will fund access to artificial intelligence tools for African creators, with the goal of helping them produce high-quality content faster and more cheaply.

item.title: Kganyago Signals South Africa Could Hike Rates Again This Month
item.description: South African Reserve Bank Governor Lesetja Kganyago said inflation expectations have risen above the central bank’s 3% target, justifying the May interest-rate increase and signaling further tightening may be needed when policymakers meet later this month.

item.title: Vietnam Retailer Dien May Xanh Raises Over $505 Million in IPO
item.description: Dien May Xanh Investment JSC, an electronics retail chain unit of Mobile World Investment Corp., raised over 13.3 trillion dong ($505 million) in its initial public offering after receiving fewer bids for shares offered.

item.title: South Africa Factory Mood Dips as Iran War Deal Delays Purchases
item.description: A gauge measuring sentiment among South African manufacturers slipped back into contractionary territory in June as customers delayed purchases in anticipation of prices declining after the US and Iran agreed to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Trans-Oil Seeks to Calm Bond Investors Rattled by Ukraine Report
item.description: Trans-Oil Group is organizing calls with bondholders rattled by a report alleging that the founder of the Moldovan grains company breached sanctions on Iran.

item.title: Pakistan Inflation Cools, Keeping Central Bank on Guard
item.description: Pakistan’s inflation eased last month, though it remained in double digits, keeping policymakers on guard against persistent price pressures.

item.title: Euro-Zone Inflation Slows More Than Expected as Oil Retreats
item.description: Euro-area inflation eased more than anticipated as efforts toward peace in the Middle East sent global oil prices lower.

item.title: ByteDance Picks Brazil for Its Largest Data Center Outside China
item.description: The TikTok creator’s $39 billion data center is the latest payoff from Lula’s courtship of Chinese investment.

item.title: The Wall Street Women Who Traded Finance Careers for Influencer Success
item.description: These pros ditched spreadsheets and trading floors to go after brand deals and social media fame.

item.title: Gilt Market Is Giving Burnham a Pass For Now, Says Aegon’s Lynch
item.description: Gilt markets have shrugged off the lack of economic policy clarity from Andy Burnham, the likely successor to replace Keir Starmer as UK prime minister, according to Aegon Asset Management’s James Lynch.

item.title: ECB’s Kocher Says the Next Decisions Will Be Either Hike or Hold
item.description: The European Central Bank’s next policy decisions will come down to either hiking interest rates further or holding them steady, Governing Council member Martin Kocher said.

item.title: Germany’s Pension Fund Would Be Safe With Bundesbank, Nagel Says
item.description: Germany’s central bank is well positioned to oversee the country’s planned state-backed pension fund if it’s asked to do so, according to President Joachim Nagel.

item.title: Alphabet’s $2 Trillion Gain Turns ‘Rock Star’ Into Question Mark
item.description: Alphabet Inc. has had a remarkable 12 months by just about any measure you can imagine.

item.title: ECB’s Nagel Says He’s Keeping Options Open for Next Two Meetings
item.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member Joachim Nagel said he’s keeping an open mind on what action is needed at the next two interest-rate meetings, with uncertainly remaining over the situation in the Middle East and its effect on inflation.

item.title: Galderma Slumps After FDA Turns Down Botox Rival Relfydess
item.description: Galderma Group AG shares slumped the most in more than a year after the US Food and Drug Administration turned down the Swiss dermatology firm’s rival Botox treatment Relfydess.
